candidates.evidence_quote: because we do not operate these restaurants directly, we can provide no assurance that our licensees will adhere to our operating standards to the same extent as we would
exchange rate fluctuations

currency fluctuations, trade restrictions, taxes or tariffs adversely affecting our or our licensees’ ability to import goods from the United States and other parts of the world that are required for operating our branded restaurants
rising cost and scarcity of labor world-wide
rising cost and scarcity of labor world-wide
inability to obtain, at a reasonable cost, adequate and reliable supplies of ingredients and products necessary to execute our diverse menu
may affect our ability to transport adequate levels of our bakery products to our or our licensees’ international restaurants, for which we are the sole source of supply
trade restrictions, import and export embargos, governmental shutdowns and disruptions in shipping
trade restrictions, import and export embargos, governmental shutdowns and disruptions in shipping
Our ability to grow our business depends on the availability and selection of high-quality sites that meet our criteria
which has become more challenging as we are experiencing longer than usual governmental delays in the licensing and permitting process
timing of the delivery of the leased premises to us from our landlords in order to perform build-out construction activities, which contributed to delays in opening new restaurants in 2023
the availability and/or cost of raw materials and labor used in construction
delays in opening new restaurants due to delays in permitting and landlord readiness, as well as supply chain challenges
complexities associated with combining independent companies with separate businesses, customers, employees, cultures and systems
damaging our reputation if retail products bearing our brand are not of the same value and quality that our customers associate with our brand
During fiscal 2023, we recorded impairment of assets and lease terminations expense of $29.5 million primarily related to the impairment of long-lived assets

We test our goodwill and other indefinite-lived intangible assets for impairment annually or on an interim basis if events or changes in circumstances between annual tests indicate a potential impairment
future impairment charges and/or closures may occur if individual restaurant performance does not improve
When we are unable to achieve sales in a sufficient amount to generate percentage rent obligations, we are not able to fully recoup available allowances at affected sites
In recent years, we have experienced an increase in wage and hour litigation
Legal proceedings, including class or collective actions can be expensive and disruptive
Several factors may significantly increase our self-insurance costs, such as conditions of the insurance market, the availability of insurance, or changes in applicable regulations
claims by customers, staff members and others regarding issues such as food-borne illness, food safety, premises liability, dram shop liability
our corporate headquarters in Calabasas, California, an area that is prone to natural disasters such as earthquakes and wildfires
A closure of or material damage to one or both of our bakery facilities could impede our ability to supply bakery products to our own and our international licensees’ restaurants
Unfavorable ESG ratings could lead to negative investor sentiment towards us or our industry, which could negatively impact our share price as well as our access to and cost of capital
we may be subject to various disclosure requirements (such as information on greenhouse gas emissions, climate risks, use of offsets, and emissions reduction claims) from the State of California
We and our third-party vendors have experienced Cybersecurity Incidents and we expect such attacks and incidents to continue in varying degrees
the California Consumer Privacy Act (“CCPA”) became effective on January 1, 2020, which created individual privacy rights for California residents
Our actual or perceived failure to comply with the PCI DSS can subject us to fines, termination of banking relationships, and increased transaction fees
Any failure to maintain financial covenants under the Loan Agreement or to have sufficient liquidity to either repay or refinance the then outstanding balance at expiration of the Loan Agreement

notes: Single-event chunk: one 10-K Risk Factors part (part 5 of 6) of filing 0001104659-24-027565 dated 2024-02-26; no fiscal_kpis present in this chunk.
Bundled sentences split per R2 (one driver per causal variable): labor_cost + labor_availability share one quote; government_shutdown + shipping_disruption share one quote.

Folded near-duplicates within chunk: ingredient cost folded into ingredient_supply; IT system failure folded into cybersecurity_incident; insurance market conditions folded into self_insurance_cost; brand goodwill dilution folded into brand_reputation; TCPA/telemarketing suits folded into class_action_litigation.

Skipped as vague or non-causal boilerplate (9): political instability/war/social unrest bundle, anti-American sentiment, consumer shopping trends, landlord financial viability, qualified tradespeople availability, adverse construction weather, FCPA/anti-boycott compliance, infrastructure scaling/sales deleveraging, online ordering and loyalty program reliance.
All evidence quotes verified as exact substrings of the event content.
